steps.title: Identify a common factor
steps.content: Every term in $6x + 12 = 24$ is divisible by 2.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — ‘$3x + 7 = 12$’: Divides $6x$ and $24$ by 2 correctly but changes 12 to 7 instead of $12 \div 2 = 6$. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘$x + 6 = 4$’: Divides $6x$ and $24$ by 6 but leaves the constant as 6 instead of $12 \div 6 = 2$. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘$5x + 4 = 8$’: Changes $12$ and $24$ as if dividing by 3, but no single operation on $6x+12=24$ produces $5x+4=8$. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘$3x + 6 = 12$’: Dividing every term by 2 gives $3x + 6 = 12$, which is equivalent to the original. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
